mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns (out-of-sample hit rate < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle expansions (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real discount rates and forces expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor market tightening and wage growth, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no predictive power for 20-day SPX direction (hit rate ~50% across all regimes).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, reducing near-term corporate output and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns during late-cycle expansions (hit rate < 45%).
